name: Narahari Tirtha
dates_notes: Direct disciple of Madhva and third pontiff of the Mādhva pīṭha-paramparā after Padmanābha-Tīrtha. Inscriptions in the Telugu region (Simhachalam temple complex) attest his Vaiṣṇava activity as Eastern-Gaṅga court official prior to formal sannyāsa.

dates_evidence.description: Simhachalam temple inscriptions (Eastern-Gaṅga period) referring to Narahari as Vaiṣṇava administrator before his pontifical succession.

core_thesis:
Narahari-Tīrtha is the third pontiff of the Mādhva *paramparā* and the figure through whom Madhva's *Gītā-Bhāṣya* receives its first sub-commentary, the *Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa*. The doctrinal architecture is Madhva's, transmitted without revision: Viṣṇu-as-Kṛṣṇa as the *svatantra-tattva* and the speaker of the *Gītā* (*Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* on *Bhagavad-Gītā* 2.12, on the *jīva-bheda*-passage); the *jīva*'s status as *paratantra* and as *aṃśa* of Viṣṇu in the *bimba-pratibimba* sense (*Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* on *Bhagavad-Gītā* 15.7, on the *mamaivāṃśo jīvaloke* verse); the *Tāratamya* (*Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* on *Bhagavad-Gītā* 14.18, on the threefold *guṇa*-graded destinies, read through the *Tāratamya*); *bhakti* as the operative means with *jñāna* and *karma* subsumed within it (*Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* on *Bhagavad-Gītā* 18.65–66, on the *carama-śloka*-pair).

The contribution is in two registers. First, the *Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* — the school's earliest sub-commentary on Madhva's *Gītā-Bhāṣya*, fixing the lineage's reading of the *Bhagavad-Gītā* in the form transmitted in the first generation. The work is shorter than Jayatīrtha's later *Prameya-Dīpikā* (which will become the standard sub-commentary for higher study) and is closely word-locked to Madhva's *Bhāṣya*-text. Second, the institutional-historical: Narahari-Tīrtha is the lineage's principal eastern-Indian agent, holding administrative office under the Eastern Gaṅga dynasty in the Telugu region (the Simhachalam temple-complex bears inscriptions from his administrative period) and recovering — by Mādhva tradition — the *mūla-vigraha* of Mūla-Rāma and Mūla-Sītā that becomes the principal cult-image of the Mādhva *pīṭha*-succession (the *Mūla-Rāma-vigraha* tradition is fixed in the lineage's transmission to Akṣobhya-Tīrtha and Jayatīrtha). His significance is therefore the geographical extension of the *paramparā* into the eastern Telugu-Oḍiyā region and the institutional fixing of the *pīṭha*-cult in the form transmitted by the later school.

engaged_works.title: Gita-Bhashya-Vivarana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Madhva's Gītā-Bhāṣya; preserved in the Mādhva manuscript canon.

ascription_evidence.description: Manuscript colophons across the Mādhva-paramparā preservation tradition.

engaged_works.summary: The *Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* is Narahari-Tīrtha's sub-commentary on Madhva's *Gītā-Bhāṣya* — the school's earliest sub-commentary on the *Gītā*-commentary in the first generation of Madhva's disciples. The method is verse-by-verse exposition with the school's settled position fixed at each contested point. Three loci do the load-bearing work. (i) The *jīva-bheda*-passages (*Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* on *Bhagavad-Gītā* 2.11–30, especially the *na tv evāhaṃ jātu nāsam* verse) fix the school's realist reading of the *jīvas*' eternal plurality, against Śaṅkara's reading of *Bhagavad-Gītā* 2.12 as licensing identity at the deeper level. (ii) The *mamaivāṃśo* verse (*Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* on *Bhagavad-Gītā* 15.7) is read in the *bimba-pratibimba* sense with strict asymmetry — the *jīva* as image owing everything to Viṣṇu as original, against any reciprocal or pariṇāma-construal. (iii) The *carama-śloka*-pair (*Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* on *Bhagavad-Gītā* 18.65–66, on *sarva-dharmān parityajya*) is read as fixing *bhakti* as the operative means with *jñāna* and *karma* subsumed within it; the *jīva*'s surrender is recognition of Viṣṇu's *svātantrya* against the agent's *paratantrya*. The work is the form in which the first-generation Mādhva community received the *Gītā-Bhāṣya*; Jayatīrtha's later *Prameya-Dīpikā* will refine and supersede for higher study, but the *Gītā-Bhāṣya-Vivaraṇa* remains in the school's manuscript canon as the earliest direct-disciple reading of Madhva on the *Gītā*.
